title: Be Quiet!
content:
Soul, dost thou fear
    For to-day or to-morrow?
    'Tis the part of a fool
    To go seeking sorrow.
    Of thine own doing
    Thou canst not contrive them.
    'Tis He that shall give them;
    Thou may'st not outlive them.
    So why cloud to-day
    With fear of the sorrow,
    That may or may not
    Come to-morrow?
meaning:
William Arthur Dunkerley (John Oxenham)'s contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Be Quiet!"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
